pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 8 creates PROJECT_DIR at this path; verified in setup and export_result scripts
setup_task.sh line 66 creates file at PROJECT_DIR/src/main/java/com/legacy/finance/CurrencyConfig.java; verified in verifier.py and export_result.sh
setup_task.sh line 69 creates test file at PROJECT_DIR/src/test/java/com/legacy/finance/CurrencyTest.java; verified in export_result.sh
setup_task.sh lines 52-54 embed these exact Unicode symbols (U+20AC Euro, U+00A3 Pound, U+00A5 Yen); verifier.py lines 75-80 check for these symbols in file content
Task description and verifier.py line 59 both expect file to be valid UTF-8 after fix; setup_task.sh line 104-110 configures project encoding to UTF-8
Task description and verifier.py lines 94-104 check that file has LF (\n) line endings and not CRLF (\r\n); setup initially creates CRLF which task must convert to LF

privileged_info_summary: The task 'fix_file_encoding_issues@1' operates on a synthetic test project (legacy-finance-app) created by setup_task.sh. The target file CurrencyConfig.java is initially encoded in Windows-1252 with CRLF line endings to simulate a real encoding problem. It contains three currency symbols: Euro (€, U+20AC), Pound (£, U+00A3), and Yen (¥, U+00A5). The expected final state is UTF-8 encoding with LF line endings and project-wide UTF-8 configuration in .idea/encodings.xml. The task verifier confirms success when: (1) file decodes as valid UTF-8, (2) all three symbols are correctly preserved, (3) line endings are LF not CRLF, (4) project encoding is configured to UTF-8, and (5) Maven unit tests pass.
